content.full_text:
CITY OF NEWPORT BEACH
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
P.O. BOX 1768, NEWPORT BEACH, CA 92659-1768
(714) 644-3131

March 16, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting Chief Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 700
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Formal Written Advice
John W. Hedges, Member of the Newport Beach City Council

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This letter is a request for formal written advice pursuant to
Section 83114(b) of the Government Code and Section 18329 of the
Regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission. I have
been authorized by John W. Hedges, a member of the Newport Beach
City Council, to make this request on his behalf and the request is
within my duties as specified in the Newport Beach City Charter.
This request contains all material facts known to Mr. Hedges and
me. In addition, I have contacted two real estate appraisers who
specialize in valuing property in the area relevant to this inquiry
and their comments are made a part of this request.

John Hedges is currently a member of the Newport Beach City
Council. Mr. Hedges was elected to this office in November, 1990.
The Newport Beach City Charter provides that Councilmembers are
nominated from, and run for office as representative of, one of
seven districts, but are elected at large. At all times relevant
to this request, Mr. Hedges has been a resident and representative
of the First Councilmanic District. The First Councilmanic
District includes the Balboa Peninsula and Lido Isle.

Mr. Hedges's principal residence is 2134 Miramar, Balboa,
("Residence") and he also owns property at 2914 West Oceanfront,
Newport Beach ("Rental House"). The Rental House is a two-story
duplex on a 25 foot by 90 foot lot. The structure consists of
approximately 3,000 square feet of floor area. The Rental House
was purchased for $460,000 in 1986 and is currently valued at
approximately $1.05 million dollars. The Rental House is leased on
a Winter/Summer basis. During the Summer, the Rental House is
leased on a weekly basis at $3,000 per week. Between Summers, the
Rental House is leased on a long term basis with the exception of
one week when it is kept vacant for repairs and maintenance.

The Rental House is immediately adjacent to a 12 foot sidewalk
commonly known as the Oceanfront Boardwalk or Oceanfront Walk
("Boardwalk"). The Residence is approximately 1500 feet from the
Boardwalk. The Boardwalk is heavily used by pedestrians,

3300 Newport Boulevard, Newport Beach

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
March 16, 1992
Page 2.


bicyclists, skaters and skateboarders.  The Boardwalk was
constructed in 1930 and presently accommodates as many as 600 to
800 users per hour.

     In August, 1991, an Orange County jury awarded $270,000 to a
person who, while walking on the Boardwalk, suffered a broken leg
when struck by a bicyclist.  This award and two large claims which
have been filed since August have sparked renewed interest in
widening the Boardwalk and/or constructing a separate bicycle
facility further out on the beach.  The Boardwalk extends for
more than 2-1/2 miles and in some areas, the beach is 500 feet or
more in width.  However, the beach is probably not wide enough in
the area near the Rental House to accommodate a separate bicycle
facility.

     I have consulted with two MAI appraisers familiar with the
value of property in this area and have given them the information
contained in this letter.  One appraiser believes there is no way
to determine if the construction of a separate bike path or wider
Boardwalk would have any impact on the value of the rental house.
The second appraiser believes that the impact on value could be
determined, that the analysis of impact would be somewhat complex
and that a wider sidewalk could slightly diminish the value of the
Rental House.  Both appraisers were convinced that changes in the
speed limit on the Boardwalk, the introduction of walk zones, and
other measures to control traffic would have no impact on the value
of any property along the Boardwalk.

     There are approximately 320 residential properties similar to
the Rental House that are adjacent to the Boardwalk.  There are
approximately 55 commercial parcels and a multi-unit condominium
development also adjacent to the Boardwalk.

     The City Council has appointed a committee to study ways to
reduce the risk of City liability for injuries sustained by
Boardwalk users.  As a matter of practice, the Councilmember
representing any district involved in such study would be appointed
to the committee as a chairperson.  Councilmember Hedges was
designated chairperson of that committee.  The committee
divided its task into two phases--the first being short term
measures to improve the flow of traffic and improve user safety.
The committee's recommendations including the adoption of speed
limits, the implementation of walk zones, and revisions to traffic
flow, were approved by the City Council in January.  The committee
is prepared to embark upon the second phase of its charge--the
development of various long term measures such as the construction

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
March 16, 1992
Page 3.


of a separate bike trail, the widening of the existing facility
and a slight oceanward relocation of the existing Boardwalk with a
sand landscape buffer between the landscape and residential
parcels.

The City Council committee will spend approximately 6 months
analyzing alternatives. The committee will then prepare a report
to the City Council which will contain recommendations. The
Council will then determine if it wants to process any of the
alternatives after receiving input from residents and community
leaders. The Council will conduct more than a "significant
substantive review" of committee recommendations.¹ Any proposal
to widen the Boardwalk or construct a separate bike path will
require amendments to the LCP and General Plan, as well as
preparation of an EIR.

Mr. Hedges would like formal written advice on the following:

1. Does he have a financial interest in any decision to widen the
Boardwalk adjacent to, or construct a separate bike path
oceanward of the Rental House that would require
disqualification pursuant to the Political Reform Act?

2. Assuming Mr. Hedges has a disqualifying economic interest,
would Mr. Hedges' involvement with the Council committee
designated to study this issue constitute participation in the
making of a governmental decision as that term is defined in
the Act?

Very truly yours,

Robert H. Burnham
City Attorney

RHB:kmc
cc: Councilmember John Hedges


¹ The Council commissioned a similar study in 1986 and received a report recommending the
construction of a separate bike path in certain areas and a wider Boardwalk in the area near the Rental House.
The consultant's recommendations were vehemently opposed by a large number of persons living on or near the
Boardwalk. The Council rejected the consultant's recommendations as a result of this political opposition and
instead approved a sidewalk repair program.

sections.question:
1. Does he have a financial interest in any decision to widen the Boardwalk adjacent to, or construct a separate bike path oceanward of the Rental House that would require disqualification pursuant to the political Reform Act?

2. Assuming Mr. Hedges has a disqualifying economic interest, would Mr. Hedges's involvement with the Council committee designated to study this issue constitute participation in the making of a governmental decision as that term is defined in the Act?
sections.question_synthetic: Does Newport Beach City Councilmember John Hedges have a disqualifying financial interest under the Political Reform Act regarding potential decisions to widen the adjacent Boardwalk or construct a separate bike path near his rental property, and if so, would his committee involvement constitute prohibited participation in such governmental decisions?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion/advice section present. Document contains detailed factual background about the rental property, its valuation, the Boardwalk situation, and the committee's work. Two specific questions posed by requestor are clearly delineated.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Does he have a financial interest in any decision to widen the Boardwalk adjacent to, or construct a separate bike path oceanward of the Rental House that would require disqualification pursuant to the political Reform Act?

2. Assuming Mr. Hedges has a disqualifying economic interest, would Mr. Hedges's involvement with the Council committee designated to study this issue constitute participation in the making of a governmental decision as that term is defined in the Act?

embedding.summary: City Attorney of Newport Beach requests formal written advice on behalf of Councilmember John Hedges regarding potential conflicts of interest arising from his ownership of a rental property adjacent to the Oceanfront Boardwalk and his appointment as chairperson of a committee studying improvements to the Boardwalk.
